member.name: Sarah Jones

addresses: Unit 2150, Level 3, The Management Suite, The Whitgift Centre, Croydon, CR0 1LP

member.synopsis: Sarah Jones is the Labour MP for Croydon West , and has been an MP continually since 8 June 2017. She currently holds the Government post of Minister of State (Home Office).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, pursuant to the Answer of 30 January 2024 to Question 10758 on Armed Forces, if he will name the regiments in the (a) Army and (b) Royal Marines which have been (i) disbanded and (ii) merged since 2010.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Further to the table provided to you in my letter of 7 February, the British Army regiments which have been disbanded since 2010 have been listed below. As noted in that letter, this information represents single Service estimates based on management info...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Members: Written Questions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, pursuant to the Answers of 24 January 2024 to Questions 10872 and 10873 on Ranger Regiment, the Answers of 24 January 2024 to Questions 10657 and 10658 on Armed Forces: Recruitment, the Answer of 24 January 2024
writtenQuestions.answerText: I will respond to the hon. Member shortly.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Modular Reactors: Cumbria
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment her Department made of the adequacy of proposals from Solway Community Power Company for the development of small modular reactors at Moorside; how much land is required for t
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has had multiple engagements with Community Power Limited but has not at present made any assessment on their proposal. The Government launched the Alternative Routes to Market for New Nuclear Projects consultation on the 11th January 2024....
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Cumbria
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what the decision-making process is for the potential release of land by the Nuclear Development Authority for nuclear development sites at Moorside.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Moorside is a candidate for new nuclear and one of a number of potential sites that could host civil nuclear projects. Whilst Great British Nuclear is working with the Government to support access to potential sites, no decisions have been made at present...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Cumbria
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what recent assessment her Department has made of the viability of Moorside as a nuclear development site; and whether her Department has had recent discussions with Sellafield Ltd on the lan
writtenQuestions.answerText: Moorside is a candidate for new nuclear and one of a number of potential sites that could host civil nuclear projects. Whilst Great British Nuclear is working with the Government to support access to potential sites, no decisions have been made at present...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Cumbria
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment her Department has made of the extent of regulatory support for potential nuclear power developments at Moorside.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There is currently no project at Moorside, but any new developer will need to meet the requirements of our independent regulators. The Government’s Nuclear Roadmap has set out plans to streamline future nuclear development to make Britian the best place i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Cumbria
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, how many developers have expressed an interest in the development of new nuclear power at Moorside since its selection as a site for new nuclear power generation in 2009.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Moorside is a candidate for new nuclear and one of a number of potential sites that could host civil nuclear projects. The specific number of enquiries is currently not known, since the NDA, as owners of the Moorside site and other land, continue to engag...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Cumbria
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether her Department has had recent discussions with (a) Sellafield Ltd and (b) the Nuclear Development Authority on its plans for nuclear power developments at Moorside.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Moorside is a candidate for new nuclear and one of a number of potential sites that could host civil nuclear projects. Whilst Great British Nuclear is working with government to support access to potential sites, no decisions have been made at present. In...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Cumbria
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what estimate her Department has made of the potential impact of the cancellation of the NuGen development at Moorside on (a) electricity generation capacity, (b) investment, (c) the number o
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 8 November 2018 Toshiba announced it would wind-up NuGen, the UK developer set to deliver a new nuclear project at Moorside. This was a commercial decision for the company. This was not a Government project and so the Department has not assessed econom...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Cumbria
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, how much financial support the Government (a) agreed to give to support the NuGen project in Moorside over the course of its proposed development and (b) gave to the project before it was can
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 8 November 2018 Toshiba announced it would wind-up NuGen, the UK developer set to deliver a new nuclear project at Moorside. This was a commercial decision for the company. There was no Government financial support given to the project.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Hinkley Point C Power Station
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of the total (a) investment, (b) number of directly-created jobs and (c) number of supply-chain jobs at Hinkley C on the econom
writtenQuestions.answerText: Hinkley Point C (HPC) is not a government project and so the Department has not assessed economic impact. EDF have advised that 19,250 job opportunities have been created on site so far and £5.3bn spent directly with companies in the South West. Further, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Sizewell C Power Station
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of the (a) total investment, (b) number of directly-created jobs and (c) number of supply-chain jobs forecast for the Sizewell
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has published an interim assessment of the Sizewell C’s project value for money, as part of the Secretary of State’s reasons for designating the project to use the Regulated Asset Base model for nuclear, and is working closely with the proj...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners' Transfers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many Prison Transfer Agreements were in place in each year since 2010.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The removal of Foreign National Offenders is a Government priority and my department continues to work closely with the Home Office to maximise the number of deportations.The total number of voluntary and compulsory Prisoner Transfer Agreements agreed sin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Reoffenders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many offenders who went on to commit a serious further offence were classified as (a) high risk, (b) medium risk and (c) low risk in each year since 2010.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The table below sets out the total number of convictions, where an offender subject to probation supervision was charged with a serious further offence (SFO), which resulted subsequently in a conviction for an SFO, for all cases notified to what is now HM...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence Equipment: Lost Property and Theft
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how much military equipment of each equipment type has been (a) lost and (b) stolen in each year since 2015.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence has historically responded to Freedom of Information Requests (FOIs), answering the following three questions: 1. How many weapons (by type) and explosive munitions for which the Department has responsibility have been recorded as ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Ammunition: Theft
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how much ammunition of each ammunition type has been stolen in each year since 2015.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to 10876 on 30 January 2024.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Ammunition: Theft
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how much of each munition type was (a) lost and (b) stolen from his Department's facilities in each year since 2015.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The security of ammunition is taken very seriously within the Ministry of Defence (MOD), and we have robust procedures in place to deter and prevent loss and theft.Given the frequency and dynamic nature of military training, exercises and operations it is...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Ammunition: Theft
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how much of each ammunition type was lost from his Department's facilities in each year since 2015.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The security of ammunition is taken very seriously within the Ministry of Defence (MOD), and we have robust procedures in place to deter and prevent loss and theft.Given the frequency and dynamic nature of military training, exercises and operations it is...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence Equipment: Theft
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, if he will publish a list of all (a) vehicles and (b) heavy weapons that have been reported missing from his Department's facilities in each year since 2015.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I will write to the hon. Member, and a copy of my letter will be placed in the Library of the House.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Ranger Regiment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what the (a) recruitment target and (b) actual number of joiners was to the Ranger Regiment for each year since its creation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is taking time to collate the required information to answer the hon. Member's questions. I will write to her when the information is available, and a copy of this letter will be placed in the Library of The House.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence
